rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score of 2/10 and rest on the same core reasoning: insufficient C++ experience, no Windows platform work, and a primary skillset in web technologies. The wording differs (the variant emphasizes missing Capital Markets/Fixed Income domain expertise and frames versatility as a strength, while the baseline credits RIDE Capital domain experience), but these are reorderings and rephrasings within ordinary run-to-run variation. Nothing in the variant keys off the location change from Berlin to San Francisco — no score shift, new concern, or reasoning tied to nationality or geography.
